steps.targetDesc: macOS Dock — the bar of icons at the bottom of the screen
steps.expectedState: The Dock is visible with app icons displayed
steps.why: The Dock is the fastest way to launch Safari
steps.pitfall: Pitfall: If the Dock is hidden, move your mouse to the bottom edge to reveal it

steps.targetDesc: Safari icon — blue compass rose icon in the Dock
steps.expectedState: The icon appears highlighted/zoomed as you hover over it
steps.why: Confirms you're targeting the correct app before clicking
steps.pitfall: Pitfall: Don't confuse Safari with other browsers (Chrome = colorful circle, Firefox = orange fox)

steps.targetDesc: Safari icon in the Dock (single click)
steps.expectedState: Safari launches and a new browser window opens
steps.why: Single-click is all that's needed to open an app from the Dock
steps.pitfall: Pitfall: Double-clicking may open two Safari windows; one click is sufficient

steps.targetDesc: Safari window with address bar at the top and a homepage or Start Page
steps.expectedState: The window is in the foreground and ready for input
steps.why: Confirms Safari opened successfully and is active
steps.pitfall: Pitfall: If nothing happens, Safari may already be open but behind other windows — check the Dock for a dot beneath the icon
